status: draft
shortDescription: "Recursive computations do not necessarily need a base case"
shortCorrection: "Recursive computations need a base case to terminate"
concepts:

related:
- id: BaseCaseSelfRecursive
#   relation: Special case
inOtherPl:
- pl: Scratch
  id: BaseCaseNotNeeded
# - pl: Python
#   id: BaseCaseNotNeeded
spec:
# - id: jls13

plVersion:
# from: jls8

---
A recursive computation does not necessarily need a base case.
Methods can directly or indirectly call themselves
without any of them including a decision to terminate the recursion.

frontmatter.shortDescription: Recursive computations do not necessarily need a base case
frontmatter.shortCorrection: Recursive computations need a base case to terminate
